description: Phase 3 of Learning Curation: curate dedup subcommand — LLM-powered semantic deduplication and merging of learnings. Finds duplicate clusters via Claude, creates consolidated merged learnings with union metadata and summed bandit stats, soft-archives originals. PREREQUISITE: Phase 1 (tasks/curate-learnings-p1.json) must be merged to main before starting this phase.

priorityPhilosophy.description: The hierarchy of what matters most when implementing tasks
hierarchy: 1. PLAN - Anticipate edge cases. Tests verify boundaries work correctly
2. FUNCTIONING CODE - Pragmatic, reliable code that works according to the plan
3. CORRECTNESS - Code compiles, type-checks, passes all tests deterministically
4. CODE QUALITY - Clean code, good patterns, no warnings
principles: Ship solid working code first, then use tests to improve it
Test the boundaries and exceptions - edge cases are where bugs hide
Handle Option/Result explicitly; avoid unwrap() in production code

globalAcceptanceCriteria.description: These criteria apply to ALL implementation tasks
criteria: Rust: No warnings in `cargo check` output
Rust: No warnings in `cargo clippy` output
Rust: All tests pass with `cargo test`
No breaking changes to existing APIs unless explicitly required

priorityGuidelines.medium: 21-50: Fix in current phase if time permits

userStories.title: Initial tests for dedup cluster merge logic
userStories.description: Write tests BEFORE implementation for the core merge_cluster() function: given a set of source learnings and merged content, verify the merged learning is created correctly with union metadata, summed bandit stats, highest confidence, and source learnings are soft-archived.
acceptanceCriteria: Test: merging 2 learnings creates 1 new learning with combined content from LLM
Test: merged learning has union of applies_to_files from all sources (no duplicates)
Test: merged learning has union of applies_to_task_types from all sources
Test: merged learning has union of applies_to_errors from all sources
Test: merged learning has union of tags from all sources (no duplicates)
Test: merged learning times_shown = sum of source times_shown values
Test: merged learning times_applied = sum of source times_applied values
Test: merged learning confidence = highest from cluster (high > medium > low)
Test: all source learnings have retired_at set after merge
Test: merged learning has retired_at = NULL (is active)
Test: merged learning has window_shown=0 and window_applied=0 (window stats reset per US-003, not carried from sources)
Test: merged learning confidence is computed from source confidences (highest wins), NOT from LLM response
Test: same learning in multiple clusters — second cluster skips already-merged learning
Known-bad discriminator: naive implementation that creates merged learning but forgets to sum bandit stats (leaves at 0,0) — test must catch this
Test file compiles (tests may be #[ignore] or expected to fail until FEAT-004)
userStories.notes: Focus on the pure DB merge logic, not the LLM interaction. Use in-memory SQLite with schema+migrations. Create learnings with known metadata and stats, then verify merge output. The merge function takes pre-validated cluster data (source IDs + merged content) and executes the DB operations.
edgeCases: Same learning in multiple dedup clusters — process clusters in order, skip learnings already merged/retired
Merging learnings where one has applies_to_files and other has NULL — union should include the non-NULL values
Merging learnings with overlapping tags — union should deduplicate
Merging learnings with different confidence levels — must use highest (high > medium > low)
Cluster of 1 learning — edge case, should be skipped (nothing to merge)
Summed times_shown overflows i32 — unlikely but document assumption
invariants: After merge: exactly 1 new learning created per valid cluster
After merge: all source learnings have retired_at IS NOT NULL
After merge: merged learning has retired_at IS NULL
After merge: merged times_shown = SUM(source times_shown), merged times_applied = SUM(source times_applied)
After merge: merged confidence >= MAX(source confidences)

failureModes.cause: Bandit stats not copied to merged learning (left at default 0,0)
failureModes.expectedBehavior: Test asserts merged times_shown > 0 when sources had non-zero stats

failureModes.cause: Source learning not retired after merge
failureModes.expectedBehavior: Test asserts all source IDs have retired_at IS NOT NULL
Test asserts tag count equals unique tag count across sources

userStories.title: Initial tests for dedup LLM prompt building and response parsing
userStories.description: Write tests for the LLM prompt construction (injection protection, content formatting) and response parsing (JSON extraction, cluster validation, error handling).
acceptanceCriteria: Test: prompt contains random UUID boundary delimiter (injection protection)
Test: prompt contains UNTRUSTED warning for learning content
Test: prompt includes all learning IDs, titles, and content
Test: prompt includes threshold value as guidance
Test: valid JSON response parses to Vec<RawDedupCluster> correctly
Test: response with non-existent learning IDs — invalid IDs filtered out, valid clusters preserved
Test: response with same learning in multiple clusters — first cluster wins, later skipped
Test: non-JSON response returns empty clusters (best-effort, no crash)
Test: markdown-wrapped JSON (```json ... ```) is extracted correctly
Test: empty array response returns 0 clusters
Known-bad discriminator: response with cluster containing only 1 ID should be rejected (not a merge)
Test file compiles
userStories.notes: Follow the testing pattern from src/learnings/ingestion/extraction.rs tests. The prompt builder and parser are pure functions — easy to test without DB or LLM. Focus on edge cases in JSON parsing and cluster validation.
edgeCases: LLM returns non-JSON / garbage — log warning, increment llm_errors counter, return empty clusters
LLM references non-existent learning IDs — validate all IDs against active learning set before acting; skip invalid clusters
LLM returns cluster with only 1 source ID — skip (not a valid merge cluster)
LLM returns empty clusters array — valid, return empty result
claude binary not found — propagate IoError with context; user sees clear error message
LLM response exceeds expected format — extract JSON array robustly (same as extraction.rs pattern)
invariants: Prompt must always contain injection protection (random delimiter + UNTRUSTED warning)
Parser must never crash on malformed LLM output — always return best-effort result
Every cluster must have >= 2 source_ids to be valid

failureModes.cause: LLM returns garbage instead of JSON
failureModes.expectedBehavior: parse_dedup_response returns Ok(empty vec), llm_errors incremented

failureModes.cause: LLM hallucinated learning IDs not in database
failureModes.expectedBehavior: validate_clusters filters out invalid IDs, logs warning per invalid cluster

userStories.title: Initial tests for dedup dry-run, re-run idempotency, and short-circuit
userStories.description: Write tests for the orchestration-level behavior: dry-run shows proposed merges without DB changes, re-running after partial completion naturally skips already-merged learnings, and 0 active learnings short-circuits without LLM call.
acceptanceCriteria: Test: dry_run=true returns DedupResult with clusters but no DB changes (no new learnings, no retired_at set)
Test: 0 active learnings returns empty DedupResult immediately (no LLM invocation)
Test: after merging cluster A, re-running excludes cluster A's source IDs (they're retired) — only remaining active learnings are processed
Test: per-cluster transaction — if cluster 2 merge fails, cluster 1 is already committed
Known-bad discriminator: dry_run=true that accidentally modifies DB — test queries learning count before/after
Test file compiles
userStories.notes: These tests exercise the curate_dedup() orchestration function. For LLM-dependent tests, mock spawn_claude via CLAUDE_BINARY env var (existing pattern). For DB-only tests, use in-memory SQLite. Focus on the invariants around dry-run safety and re-run idempotency.
edgeCases: 0 learnings in database — return empty result, no LLM invocation, no error
All active learnings are unique (no duplicates) — LLM returns empty clusters, 0 merges
Process killed between clusters — committed clusters are durable, re-run picks up remaining
Process killed mid-cluster — transaction rolls back, no partial state. LLM work lost but DB consistent
Dedup threshold too aggressive (0.1) — merges unrelated learnings, but originals recoverable via unretire
invariants: dry_run=true must NEVER modify the database (no inserts, no updates)
After re-run: total active learnings + total retired learnings = original total
Per-cluster transactions: committed clusters survive even if later clusters fail

failureModes.cause: dry_run flag not checked before DB writes
failureModes.expectedBehavior: Test verifies learning count unchanged after dry_run=true
Test verifies merged sources (retired) are excluded from second run

userStories.title: Add Dedup variant to CurateAction enum with CLI flags
userStories.description: Extend the existing CurateAction enum (created by Phase 1) to add the Dedup variant with --dry-run, --threshold, and --batch-size flags.
acceptanceCriteria: CurateAction::Dedup variant added to cli/commands.rs
Dedup has: --dry-run (bool, default false)
Dedup has: --threshold (f64, default 0.7) — merge aggressiveness
Dedup has: --batch-size (Option<usize>, None = auto) — max learnings per LLM call
task-mgr curate dedup --help shows correct flags with descriptions
--threshold value validated: clap rejects negative values or values > 1.0 with descriptive error
Compiles without warnings
userStories.notes: Phase 1 creates CurateAction with Retire and Unretire variants. This task adds the Dedup variant. Follow the exact same pattern for CLI flag definitions. The threshold is semantic guidance passed to the LLM prompt, not a computed similarity score.

correctness: Default threshold must be 0.7 (conservative to avoid merging unrelated learnings)
batch_size must be Option<usize> — None means auto-calculate from content size
style: Follow exact pattern from Phase 1's Retire variant for --dry-run flag

userStories.title: Dedup types: DedupParams, DedupResult, DedupCluster structs
userStories.description: Create the parameter, result, and intermediate types for the dedup subcommand. Add to the existing src/commands/curate/types.rs (created by Phase 1).
acceptanceCriteria: DedupParams struct: dry_run (bool), threshold (f64), batch_size (Option<usize>)
DedupResult struct: dry_run (bool), clusters_found (usize), learnings_merged (usize), learnings_created (usize), llm_errors (usize), clusters (Vec<DedupCluster>)

DedupCluster struct: source_ids (Vec<i64>), source_titles (Vec<String>), merged_title (String), merged_content (String), merged_outcome (String), merged_confidence (String), reason (String), merged_learning_id (Option<i64>)
All types derive Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize
DedupResult derives Serialize for JSON output mode
RawDedupCluster struct (serde::Deserialize only) for parsing LLM response: source_ids, merged_title, merged_content, merged_outcome, reason — all Optional for robustness
Compiles without warnings
userStories.notes: Follow the type pattern from Phase 1's RetireResult and UnretireResult. RawDedupCluster is the deserialization target from LLM JSON — fields are Optional because LLM output is untrusted. DedupCluster is the validated internal type.

correctness: RawDedupCluster fields must all be Option<T> — LLM output is untrusted and may omit fields
DedupCluster.merged_learning_id is Option<i64>: None in dry-run, Some(id) after merge
style: Follow existing type patterns: Params/Result pairs, Serialize for all result types
Place RawDedupCluster in types.rs alongside other dedup types (not in a separate file)

userStories.title: Dedup LLM prompt building and response parsing
userStories.description: Implement build_dedup_prompt() and parse_dedup_response() functions following the pattern from src/learnings/ingestion/extraction.rs. The prompt presents active learnings to Claude and asks it to identify duplicate clusters with proposed merged content. The parser extracts JSON, validates structure, and filters invalid clusters.
acceptanceCriteria: build_dedup_prompt(learnings, threshold) returns a prompt string with: random UUID delimiter, UNTRUSTED warning, all learning content, threshold guidance, expected JSON format
Prompt requests JSON array of cluster objects with: source_ids, merged_title, merged_content, merged_outcome, reason
parse_dedup_response(response, valid_ids) returns Vec<RawDedupCluster> with best-effort parsing
Parser handles: raw JSON array, markdown-wrapped JSON (```json ... ```), generic code blocks
Parser returns empty Vec on non-JSON input (no crash)
validate_clusters(raw_clusters, active_ids) filters: clusters with < 2 source_ids, clusters with non-existent IDs, learnings appearing in multiple clusters (first wins)
All TEST-INIT-002 tests pass
userStories.notes: Follow extraction.rs pattern exactly for injection protection and JSON parsing. The prompt should instruct Claude to evaluate semantic similarity at the given threshold level. Include the learning's ID, title, content, outcome, and tags in the prompt so Claude has full context. Reuse the extract_json_array() pattern from extraction.rs (or factor out as shared utility if extraction.rs is accessible).

correctness: Prompt must use random UUID delimiter to prevent injection (not a predictable string)
Prompt must mark learning content as UNTRUSTED
Parser must never crash on malformed input — always return best-effort result
validate_clusters must ensure each learning ID appears in at most one cluster
performance: Truncate prompt if total learning content exceeds context limit (~150K chars)
style: Follow extraction.rs patterns: build_extraction_prompt() style, extract_json_array() style
RawDedupCluster uses all-Optional fields like RawExtractedLearning

userStories.title: Cluster merge logic: merge_cluster() function
userStories.description: Implement the core merge_cluster() function that, given a validated cluster (source IDs + merged content), executes the DB operations: create merged learning via record_learning(), update bandit stats via raw SQL, collect union metadata and tags from sources, set retired_at on all source learnings. Single transaction per cluster.
acceptanceCriteria: merge_cluster(conn, cluster, source_learnings) creates merged learning via record_learning()
Merged learning has: title/content/outcome from cluster, union of applies_to_files/task_types/errors from sources, union of tags from sources, highest confidence from sources
After creation, UPDATE times_shown and times_applied with summed values from sources (raw SQL)
Set retired_at = datetime('now') on all source learning IDs
Merged learning has window_shown=0, window_applied=0 (natural from INSERT defaults — verified, not explicitly set)
All operations in a single transaction (conn.transaction())
Returns merged learning ID on success
All TEST-INIT-001 tests pass
userStories.notes: record_learning() always sets times_shown=0, times_applied=0. After creating the merged learning, execute a raw UPDATE to set the summed stats. This is the cleanest approach that reuses existing CRUD. For metadata union: collect all applies_to_files from sources into a HashSet, then convert back to Vec. Same for task_types, errors, and tags. For tags, use the tag INSERT from record_learning() (which handles it), but ensure union is deduplicated before passing.

correctness: Bandit stats (times_shown, times_applied) MUST be summed from sources, not left at 0
Metadata union must deduplicate (HashSet) — no duplicate file paths or tags
Confidence must be highest from cluster (high > medium > low), not from LLM
All source learnings must have retired_at set — missing even one means duplicates persist
Transaction must be atomic: if any step fails, no partial merge state
performance: Load source learning tags in batch (one query per cluster, not one per source)
Single UPDATE for all source retirements (WHERE id IN (...))
style: Use record_learning() for creation, raw SQL only for stats update and retirement
Use HashSet for metadata deduplication, not manual Vec iteration

userStories.title: Dedup orchestration: curate_dedup() main function
userStories.description: Implement the curate_dedup() function that orchestrates the full dedup flow: load active learnings, auto-calculate or apply batch size, iterate batches calling spawn_claude(), parse/validate responses, iterate clusters calling merge_cluster(), track progress and errors, handle dry-run mode.
acceptanceCriteria: curate_dedup(conn, params) loads all active learnings (WHERE retired_at IS NULL)
Short-circuits with empty result if 0 active learnings (no LLM call)
Auto-calculates batch size if params.batch_size is None: single batch if total content < 150K chars, otherwise ~20 learnings per batch
For each batch: calls spawn_claude() with built prompt, parses response, validates clusters
For each valid cluster: calls merge_cluster() (or previews in dry-run mode)
dry_run=true: builds DedupResult with cluster details but no DB modifications
Prints batch progress: 'Processing batch 2/15...'
Tracks llm_errors count (non-zero exit code or parse failure)
Returns DedupResult with aggregate stats
Gracefully handles spawn_claude() failure (IoError for missing binary, non-zero exit code)
All TEST-INIT-003 tests pass
userStories.notes: This is the orchestration function that ties everything together. Follow the pattern from src/learnings/ingestion/mod.rs for LLM error handling (best-effort, continue on failure). For batch size auto-calculation: sum content.len() for all learnings; if < 150_000, single batch; otherwise divide into batches of ~20. Track already-merged IDs across batches to handle the case where a learning appears in clusters from different batches.

correctness: 0 learnings must short-circuit immediately — no LLM call, no error
dry_run must NEVER modify the database
Already-merged learning IDs must be tracked across batches and skipped in later clusters
spawn_claude() failure must not crash the operation — log error, increment counter, continue
performance: Per-cluster transactions so completed work survives interruption
Re-running after interruption naturally resumes (retired learnings excluded from load)
style: Follow ingestion/mod.rs pattern for spawn_claude() error handling
Use eprintln! for progress output (not stdout, which is for result output)

userStories.title: Dedup output formatting: format_dedup_text() + TextFormattable
userStories.description: Implement the text output formatting for DedupResult and register TextFormattable in handlers.rs.
acceptanceCriteria: format_dedup_text(result) returns human-readable text with: summary line (N clusters, M learnings merged, K created), dry-run indicator, per-cluster details (source titles, merged title, reason)
Dry-run output clearly marked: 'DRY RUN — no changes made'
Error count shown if llm_errors > 0: 'N LLM errors encountered'
Format function added to src/commands/curate/output.rs
impl_text_formattable!(DedupResult, format_dedup_text) added to handlers.rs
JSON output works via derive(Serialize) — no additional code needed
userStories.notes: Follow the formatting pattern from Phase 1's format_retire_text and format_unretire_text. Keep output concise: summary line at top, cluster details below. For dry-run, show what WOULD be merged. For non-dry-run, show what WAS merged with merged learning IDs.

correctness: DedupResult must impl Serialize (derive) for JSON output mode
impl_text_formattable! must reference the correct format function path
style: Follow Phase 1 output patterns for consistency
Dry-run output clearly distinguishable from actual execution output

userStories.title: Wire up dedup dispatch in main.rs and handlers.rs
userStories.description: Add the CurateAction::Dedup match arm in main.rs dispatch, connecting CLI flags to curate_dedup(). Add necessary imports to main.rs and handlers.rs.
acceptanceCriteria: Commands::Curate match in main.rs has CurateAction::Dedup arm
Dedup arm: acquires LockGuard, opens connection, constructs DedupParams, calls curate_dedup(), calls output_result()
Imports added: DedupParams and curate_dedup from commands/curate module
DedupResult import added to handlers.rs alongside existing curate imports
task-mgr curate dedup --dry-run works end-to-end
task-mgr curate dedup --threshold 0.8 works
JSON output mode works (--format json curate dedup --dry-run)
Dispatch propagates errors from curate_dedup() to CLI error output (no silent swallowing)
userStories.notes: Follow the exact dispatch pattern from Phase 1's Retire and Unretire arms. LockGuard is required because dedup creates and modifies learnings. The curate_dedup function takes &Connection and DedupParams.

correctness: LockGuard must be acquired before DB operations
Connection opened with &cli.dir consistently
style: Follow exact dispatch pattern from Phase 1's curate retire/unretire arms
Import new types alongside existing curate imports

userStories.title: Review dedup implementation for quality, security, and integration wiring
userStories.description: Review all Phase 3 implementation for code quality, error handling, security (especially LLM prompt injection protection), and verify all new code is fully integrated.
acceptanceCriteria: No unwrap() in production code paths
All errors properly propagated with context
LLM prompt uses random delimiter injection protection
LLM prompt marks learning content as UNTRUSTED
Response parser handles all malformed inputs gracefully (no panics)
Quality dimensions met: each task's qualityDimensions constraints satisfied
Edge case coverage: each edgeCases entry on TEST-INIT tasks has a corresponding test
New dedup module exported from curate/mod.rs
DedupResult registered with TextFormattable in handlers.rs
main.rs dispatch covers Dedup variant
No dead code warnings for new code
spawn_claude() failure handling is best-effort (no crash)

Focus areas: 1) LLM security (injection protection), 2) Transaction safety (per-cluster commits), 3) Bandit stats summing (must not lose data), 4) Integration wiring (end-to-end reachability). For each issue found: add CODE-FIX-xxx task (priority 14-16), add to MILESTONE-1 dependsOn, commit JSON changes.

userStories.title: Review dedup implementation for refactoring opportunities

acceptanceCriteria: No code duplication (DRY principle) — especially JSON parsing logic shared with extraction.rs
Functions under 30 lines (flag complex ones)
No tight coupling between dedup and internal query details
Code is easy to change and extend
Any issues found have corresponding REFACTOR-1-xxx tasks added to JSON
userStories.notes: Look especially for opportunities to share JSON extraction utilities between dedup and extraction.rs (extract_json_array, find_matching_bracket). For each issue: 1) Add REFACTOR-1-xxx (priority 18-19), 2) Add to MILESTONE-1 dependsOn, 3) Commit JSON.

userStories.title: Comprehensive tests for dedup merge edge cases
userStories.description: Expand test coverage for merge logic: metadata combinations, large clusters, confidence ordering, outcome handling, transaction isolation.
acceptanceCriteria: Test: merging 5+ learnings in a single cluster — all stats summed, all metadata unioned
Test: merging learnings where ALL metadata fields are NULL — merged learning has NULL fields (not empty arrays)
Test: merging learnings with overlapping applies_to_files — deduplication correct
Test: confidence ordering edge cases — medium + high = high, low + low = low
Test: merged outcome matches LLM-proposed outcome (not computed from sources)
Test: cluster with one source already retired (from previous cluster in same run) — skipped gracefully
Test: root_cause and solution from sources are not propagated to merged learning (content captures this)
Test: merged learning has correct created_at (now, not copied from sources)
80%+ coverage for merge_cluster function
userStories.notes: These tests expand on TEST-INIT-001 with more complex scenarios. Focus on interaction between metadata union and various NULL/non-NULL combinations.

userStories.title: Comprehensive tests for dedup LLM interaction and failure modes
userStories.description: Expand test coverage for LLM prompt/parse/orchestration: batching behavior, progress output, error accumulation, threshold variation, large corpus handling.
acceptanceCriteria: Test: batch auto-calculation — corpus > 150K chars splits into multiple batches
Test: batch_size override — explicit --batch-size 5 creates batches of 5
Test: LLM returns duplicate IDs across batches — already-merged tracking prevents double-merge
Test: multiple consecutive LLM failures — llm_errors count accurate, no crash
Test: threshold=0.0 (merge everything) — LLM receives extreme threshold, result is whatever LLM decides
Test: threshold=1.0 (merge nothing) — LLM receives extreme threshold, result likely 0 clusters
Test: progress output goes to stderr (not stdout, which is for result)
Test: JSON output mode produces valid JSON for DedupResult with all fields
80%+ coverage for curate_dedup orchestration function
userStories.notes: For LLM-dependent tests, mock spawn_claude via CLAUDE_BINARY env var pointing to a script that returns pre-canned JSON responses. This avoids real LLM calls in tests.

Test names clearly describe what is tested
